questions.prompt: Complete: "My brother usually ___ to school by bus."
questions.explanation: "usually" marks a habit, so we use the present simple; with the third-person singular subject "my brother" the verb takes -s: goes. "go" forgets the -s; "is going" is the present continuous (action now), which clashes with "usually"; "going" alone is not a finite verb.

"know" is a stative verb (a mental state), so it is not used in the continuous: I know the answer. "am knowing" wrongly makes a stative verb continuous; "I knows" adds -s to a first-person subject; "am know" mixes "be" with a base verb.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Look! It ___ outside, so take an umbrella."
questions.explanation: "Look!" shows the action is happening at this moment, so we use the present continuous: it is raining. "rains" is present simple (a habit or general truth); "rain" lacks the -s and the continuous form; "raining" alone has no auxiliary "is".

questions.prompt: Choose the correct question: "___ your sister speak English?"
questions.explanation: Present simple questions with he/she/it use the auxiliary "does" + base verb: Does your sister speak...? "Do" is for I/you/we/they; "Is"/"Are" would introduce the present continuous (Is your sister speaking...?), not the present simple.

questions.prompt: Complete: "These days, more and more people ___ remotely from home."
questions.explanation: "These days" describes a temporary trend in progress around now, so we use the present continuous; the plural subject "people" takes "are": are working. "works" wrongly adds -s to a plural subject; "is working" uses the singular "is" with a plural subject; "work" (present simple) would suggest a fixed permanent habit, not a current trend.

In the present simple negative with he, the auxiliary "does" carries the -s and the main verb stays in its base form: he doesn't understand. "don't" is the wrong auxiliary for "he"; "doesn't understands" double-marks the -s; "isn't understand" mixes "be" with a base verb.
